name: Arribas do Tejo Trail (PR1 GAV)
name_original: Arribas do Tejo (PR 1) — percurso circular com os Passadiços do Alamal
description: Hike the moderate 10.6-mile Arribas do Tejo Trail (PR1 GAV) for scenic Tagus River views, historical sites, and diverse terrain.
region_intro: Hiking around Ortiga features routes primarily along the Tagus River (Tejo), characterized by riverine landscapes and gentle elevation changes. The terrain includes paths near fluvial beaches and through rural areas, offering accessible outdoor experiences. The region provides a mix of easy and moderate trails suitable for various hiking abilities. Best hiking trails around Ortiga The most popular hiking route is Arribas do Tejo Trail (PR1 GAV), a 10.6 miles (17.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 42 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the banks of the Tagus River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Praia Fluvial do Alamal loop from Belver, an easy 2.5 miles (4.0 km) path. This trail explores the area around the fluvial beach, offering a relaxed walking experience. Local hikers also love the Parish Church of Sardoal loop from Sardoal, a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 4 minutes. Hiking around Ortiga is defined by river paths, fluvial beaches, and rural trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate excursions. The routes in Ortiga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 90 hikers have used komoot to explore Ortiga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Ortiga?
A: There are 5 hiking trails documented around Ortiga, offering a variety of experiences along the Tagus River and through rural landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Ortiga?
A: Hikes around Ortiga primarily feature riverine landscapes along the Tagus River (Tejo). You'll find paths near fluvial beaches and through rural areas, generally characterized by gentle elevation changes.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Ortiga?
A: Yes, Ortiga offers several easy trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21949755" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia Fluvial do Alamal loop from Belver&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.5-mile (4.0 km) path that explores the area around the fluvial beach, perfect for a relaxed walk.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Ortiga?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Ortiga are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32138575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish Church of Sardoal loop from Sardoal&lt;/a&gt; is a 4.5-mile (7.2 km) route that takes you through rural landscapes, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34326869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miradouro natural da Boavista loop from Barragem de Belver&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic views on a shorter, easy path.

Q: What is the average duration for hikes in Ortiga?
A: The duration of hikes in Ortiga varies depending on the trail's length and your pace. Easy trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21949755" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia Fluvial do Alamal loop from Belver&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in just over an hour, while longer, moderate routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2244731674/arribas-do-tejo-trail-pr1-gav" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arribas do Tejo Trail (PR1 GAV)&lt;/a&gt; might take around 4 hours 42 minutes.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Ortiga suitable for families?
A: Yes, with a good selection of easy trails, Ortiga is suitable for families. The gentle terrain and fluvial beaches make for pleasant outings. Shorter loop trails are particularly good for families with children.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Ortiga?
A: The hiking experiences in Ortiga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 15 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil riverine landscapes, the accessibility of the trails, and the opportunity to explore rural areas.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or natural features to look out for on the trails?
A: The trails around Ortiga are defined by their connection to the Tagus River (Tejo) and its fluvial beaches. You'll find scenic river views and peaceful rural landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34326869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miradouro natural da Boavista loop from Barragem de Belver&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights a natural viewpoint.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Ortiga?
A: While specific parking details for each trailhead are best checked on individual route pages, trails often start from accessible points in or near villages and fluvial beaches, where parking facilities are typically available for visitors.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ortiga?
A: Given the riverine and rural nature of the trails, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking in Ortiga, avoiding the heat of summer and potential winter chill. These seasons provide comfortable conditions for exploring the outdoors.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes in Ortiga?
A: For those seeking a longer, moderate challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2244731674/arribas-do-tejo-trail-pr1-gav" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arribas do Tejo Trail (PR1 GAV)&lt;/a&gt; is a 10.6-mile (17.1 km) route. It follows the banks of the Tagus River and involves more significant elevation changes compared to the easier loops.
tips: The Anta do Penedo Gordo stands like a stone echo from ancient times. With its polygonal chamber formed by nine pillars and a corridor flanked by four on each side, this megalithic monument still preserves traces of the ancient mound, visible mainly to the south. Its construction dates back to the Late Neolithic or Chalcolithic period (between 3500 and 2000 BC) when the landscape was filled with mystery, rituals and stories carved into the rock.

The historic Castle of Belver (Castelo de Belver) is one of Portugal's most beautiful castles.

It was built by the Order of the Hospitallers in the late 12th century early 13th century on the border between what was then Christian and Islamic Portugal - the River Tagus.

The castle is on a hill on the northern bank of the river. The name comes from belo ver or "beautiful to see."
good spot to go and visit

faqs.question: What is the current status of the Arribas do Tejo Trail (PR1 GAV)?
faqs.answer: It's important to note that the Municipality of Gavião has temporarily closed a number of pedestrian routes, likely including the PR1 GAV, for essential maintenance and improvement works. We recommend checking the current status with local authorities or the Municipality of Gavião before planning your trip to ensure the trail is open and safe for use.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this trail?
faqs.answer: The Arribas do Tejo Trail (PR1 GAV) is a circular route that typically takes around 4 hours and 42 minutes to complete. This estimate is for hiking and can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Arribas do Tejo Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied terrain, including natural foot trails with numerous uphill and downhill sections through untouched nature. You'll encounter paths between ancient walls, country tracks winding through old olive groves and small crop fields, and narrow paths along ridgelines. A notable part of the trail includes a wooden walkway alongside the river, a suspension bridge over the Belver Stream, and a stairway near the Belver Tapestry Museum.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You'll pass by the megalithic Anta do Penedo Gordo, the impressive medieval Belver Castle, and the beautiful river beach Praia Fluvial do Alamal. Other highlights include the fantastic refurbished Alamal Walkways, views of the Belver Dam, and a contemporary art exhibition along parts of the path.

faqs.question: Is the Arribas do Tejo Trail (PR1 GAV) suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The trail is classified as moderate in difficulty, due to both its technical demands and stamina requirements. While it features varied terrain with uphill and downhill sections, it is generally manageable for hikers with a reasonable level of fitness. Beginners who are prepared for a longer hike with some elevation changes should find it enjoyable.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, there is no explicit information regarding dog-friendliness or specific leash rules. Given the varied terrain and potential for encountering wildlife or agricultural areas, it's advisable to keep dogs on a leash and ensure they are comfortable with longer hikes. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Alentejo region can get very hot in summer. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for hiking, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Winter can also be a good option, though some sections might be muddy after rain. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Arribas do Tejo Trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to hike the Arribas do Tejo Trail (PR1 GAV). It is generally accessible to the public, though as mentioned, check for any temporary closures before your visit.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The Arribas do Tejo Trail (PR1 GAV) is a circular route, meaning you can typically start and end at the same point. While specific parking details aren't provided, trailheads for circular routes often have designated parking areas in nearby villages or points of interest. We recommend checking local maps or resources for the most convenient starting point and parking options.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, while the Arribas do Tejo trail covers 100% of its own route, it also briefly intersects with sections of other paths. You might find yourself on small portions of the Rota da Ortigal Sul, Passadiço do Alamal, Largo Luís de Camões, Caminho da Fonte Velha, and Caminho da Fonte do Chá.
